title: App Icon Resolution and Serving
interaction_flow: HTTPClient → RouteHandler: GET /app-icon/:app_uid OR /app-icon/:app_uid/:size
RouteHandler → RouteHandler: validate size (must be in [16,32,64,128,256,512]), default=128
RouteHandler → AppIconService.normalizeAppUid(appUid): prefix 'app-' if missing → normalizedAppUid
RouteHandler → AppIconService.#getIconStream({ appUid, size, allowRedirect })
#getIconStream → AppIconService.normalizeAppUid(appUid)
[IF appIcon is SVG data URL] → return { mime:'image/svg+xml', stream: buffer_to_stream(svg) }
#getIconStream → FileSystem.getChild('{appUid}-{size}.png'): legacyNode
[IF legacyNode.exists() AND allowRedirect] → AppIconService.getSizedIconUrl({appUid,size}) → return { redirectUrl, redirectCacheControl:'public, max-age=900' }
[IF legacyNode.exists() AND NOT allowRedirect] → AppIconService.readIconNodeBuffer({node:legacyNode}) → LLRead → stream_to_buffer → return { mime:'image/png', stream }
[IF legacyNode read fails AND tries<1] → recursive #getIconStream(size*2, tries+1)
[IF no legacyNode] → AppIconService.getOriginalIconLookup({ dirAppIcons, appUid }): check '{appUid}.png'
[IF originalNode exists] → AppIconService.queueMissingSizeFromOriginal({appUid, size}) [async background]
[IF originalNode exists AND allowRedirect AND isFlatOriginal] → AppIconService.getOriginalIconUrl({appUid}) → return { redirectUrl, redirectCacheControl:'public, max-age=60' }
[IF originalNode exists] → AppIconService.readIconNodeBuffer({node:originalNode}) → return { mime:'image/png', stream }
[IF no original] → getExternalRedirect(): check shouldRedirectIconUrl(appIconOriginal|dbIcon) → return { redirectUrl } if applicable
[FALLBACK] → getFallbackIcon(): Database.get_app({uid:appUid}), normalizeRawBase64ImageString(app.icon), pick appIcon||dbIcon||DEFAULT_APP_ICON → return { mime, stream }
[IF dbIcon is dataUrl during fallback] → AppIconService.queueDataUrlIconWrite({appUid, dataUrl}) [async background]
RouteHandler ← AppIconService.#getIconStream: { stream, mime, redirectUrl?, redirectCacheControl? }
[IF redirectUrl] → RouteHandler: res.set('Cache-Control', redirectCacheControl), res.redirect(302, redirectUrl)
[ELSE] → RouteHandler: res.set('Content-Type', mime), res.set('Cache-Control','public, max-age=3600'), stream.pipe(res)
HTTPClient ← RouteHandler: 302 redirect OR 200 image stream
background_flows: queueDataUrlIconWrite → createAppIcons({data}) → loadIconSource(dataUrl) → getSharp() → write original PNG + all ICON_SIZES PNGs → persistConvertedIconUrl(appUid, endpointUrl)
persistConvertedIconUrl → DB UPDATE apps SET icon WHERE uid AND icon LIKE 'data:%' → AppRedisCacheSpace.invalidateCachedApp(app) → svcEvent.emit('app.changed')

key_methods.normalizeAppUid(appUid): Returns appUid with 'app-' prefix; no-op if already prefixed
key_methods.isDataUrl(value): Returns true if string starts with 'data:' and contains ','
key_methods.shouldRedirectIconUrl({iconUrl,appUid,si: True if URL is puterSubdomain or appIconEndpoint BUT not the same endpoint as current request
key_methods.iconifyApps({apps,size}): Maps app array: sets app.icon = getAppIconPath({appUid: app.uid??app.uuid, size})
key_methods.#getIconStream(...): Private: resolves icon as stream or redirect URL using filesystem lookup with fallback chain

key_relationships: AppIconService depends_on FileSystem service (svcFs, getChild, LLRead, HLWrite, LLMkdir)
AppIconService depends_on Database service (DB_READ/DB_WRITE for 'apps' and 'sites' tables)
AppIconService depends_on su service (svcSu.sudo, get_system_actor, get_system_user)
init_flow: user.system-user-ready event → ensureAppIconsDirectory('/system/app_icons') → ensureAppIconsSubdomain() → INSERT subdomains table
important_notes: Resolution priority: (1) legacy sized PNG file, (2) original PNG file (+ queue resize), (3) external redirect (puter subdomain or endpoint URL), (4) fallback = DB app.icon or DEFAULT_APP_ICON. allowRedirect is true when config.no_subdomain is falsy. Raw base64 strings are normalized to data:image/png;base64 before processing. SVGs are returned as-is without resizing.
